description: Cross-file variant propagation. A color decision is amended mid-session (amber-500 → amber-600); a later turn must carry the AMENDED value into another file. Tests forgetting + decision retention.

turns.user:
Selected canvas node context:
- Label: Primitives · Badge
- Source path: src/components/ui/badge.tsx

User request:
Add a new `warning` variant to the badge cva: border-transparent bg-amber-500 text-white.

Selected canvas node context:
- Label: Primitives · Badge
- Source path: src/components/ui/badge.tsx

User request:
Actually make warning use bg-amber-600 instead of 500 — better contrast.

Selected canvas node context:
- Label: Primitives · Badge
- Source path: src/components/ui/badge.tsx

User request:
Also add an `info` variant: border-transparent bg-sky-600 text-white.
Now add the same `warning` variant to the button component (src/components/ui/button.tsx), using the exact classes we settled on for the badge. Don't ask — just add it.
turns.note: forgetting probe: must use amber-600, not amber-500

turns.user: Which amber shade did we settle on for warning, and why did we move off the first one? One sentence, no edits.

judgeRubric: The warning color was amended from amber-500 to amber-600 in turn 2 (reason: contrast). Turn 4 requires propagating the AMENDED value (amber-600) to button.tsx; using amber-500 there is a contradiction of the earlier decision. Turn 5 must recall both the shade and the contrast rationale. Flag redid_work if the agent re-established context it already had (e.g. re-reading badge.tsx to recover its own recent edit).
